root_text.an10.238:1.3: Aniccasaññā, anattasaññā, āhāre paṭikūlasaññā, sabbaloke anabhiratasaññā, aṭṭhikasaññā, puḷavakasaññā, vinīlakasaññā, vipubbakasaññā, vicchiddakasaññā, uddhumātakasaññā—
root_text.an10.238:1.4: rāgassa, bhikkhave, abhiññāya ime dasa dhammā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.an10.238:1.1: “For insight into greed, ten things should be developed.
translation_text.an10.238:1.3: The perceptions of impermanence, not-self, repulsiveness of food, dissatisfaction with the whole world, a skeleton, a worm-infested corpse, a livid corpse, a festering corpse,  a split open corpse, and a bloated corpse.
translation_text.an10.238:1.4: For insight into greed, these ten things should be developed.”
